question_text: Let 𝑓: (−1, 1) →ℝ be a differentiable function. Consider the following statements: (I) Suppose 𝑓(0) ≥0, and 𝑓′(𝑥) > 0 whenever 𝑓(𝑥) = 0, for any 𝑥≥0. Then 𝑓(𝑥) > 0, for any 𝑥> 0. (II) Suppose 𝑓(0) ≤0, and 𝑓′(𝑥) > 0 whenever 𝑓(𝑥) = 0, for any 𝑥≤0. Then 𝑓(𝑥) < 0, for any 𝑥< 0. Which of the following statements is correct?

options.D: Neither statement (I) nor statement (II) is correct
